children.name: RegisterPatient
children.purpose: Register a new patient in the system
children.behavior: Extract patient details from input, validate uniqueness, store in patients data store, and return success or error
postconditions: patient is stored in patients data store

call_inputs.name: input
call_inputs.description: JSON with patient registration details (name, dob, contact, etc.)

internal_leaf_accesses.reason: Check uniqueness of patient ID and store new patient record

outputs.name: output
outputs.description: JSON with success status, patient ID, and message

global_vars.description: Check uniqueness and store new patient record

data_operations.description: Check uniqueness of patient ID and store new patient record

constraints.description: Patient ID must be unique across the patients data store

acceptance_criteria.description: Given valid input, a new patient record is created and a unique ID is returned
Given duplicate patient ID, an error is returned and no record is created

children.name: BookAppointment
children.purpose: Book an appointment for a registered patient
children.behavior: Extract patient ID and appointment details, verify patient exists, check slot availability, store appointment, and return confirmation
postconditions: appointment is stored in appointments data store

call_inputs.name: input
call_inputs.description: JSON with patient ID and appointment details (date, time, doctor, etc.)

internal_leaf_accesses.reason: Check slot availability and store new appointment

outputs.name: output
outputs.description: JSON with success status, appointment ID, and message

global_vars.description: Check slot availability and store new appointment

data_operations.description: Check slot availability and store new appointment

constraints.description: Patient must exist in patients data store before booking
Appointment slot must be available (no double booking)

acceptance_criteria.description: Given valid patient and available slot, appointment is created and ID returned
Given non-existent patient, error is returned
Given already booked slot, error is returned

children.name: GetRecords
children.purpose: Retrieve medical records for a patient
children.behavior: Extract patient ID, verify patient exists, fetch records from patients data store, and return records

internal_leaf_accesses.reason: Verify patient exists and fetch medical records

outputs.name: output
outputs.description: JSON with success status, records data, and message

global_vars.description: Verify patient exists and fetch medical records

data_operations.description: Verify patient exists and fetch medical records

constraints.description: Patient must exist in patients data store
Records are read-only, no modification allowed

acceptance_criteria.description: Given valid patient ID, records are returned successfully
Given non-existent patient ID, error is returned

children.name: UpdateProfile
children.behavior: Extract patient ID and updated fields, verify patient exists, update profile in patients data store, and return confirmation
preconditions: input contains valid fields to update
postconditions: patient profile is updated in patients data store

call_inputs.name: input
call_inputs.description: JSON with patient ID and updated profile fields (name, contact, etc.)

internal_leaf_accesses.reason: Verify patient exists and update profile fields

outputs.name: output
outputs.description: JSON with success status, updated profile, and message

global_vars.description: Verify patient exists and update profile fields

data_operations.description: Verify patient exists and update profile fields

constraints.description: Patient must exist in patients data store before update
Only specified fields are changed, others remain unchanged

acceptance_criteria.description: Given valid patient ID and fields, profile is updated and confirmation returned
Given non-existent patient ID, error is returned

governance_notes: All children are atomic operations (or pure function for GetRecords) that directly access data stores. No further decomposition is needed. Resource fields are derived directly from internal_leaf_accesses. global_vars and data_operations are synchronized for each child. Requested capabilities follow the pattern resource.operation. Constraints and acceptance criteria are added to ensure data integrity and atomicity.

resource_audit.signature_preservation_detail: All signatures are preserved exactly as provided in Stage 2. No changes were made to call_inputs, outputs, or signature fields.
resource_audit.internal_leaf_mapping_detail: Each child's internal_leaf_accesses are directly mapped to global_vars and data_operations. For example, RegisterPatient's 'patients read_write' appears in both global_vars and data_operations. BookAppointment's two accesses (patients read, appointments read_write) are both mapped.
resource_audit.global_vars_data_operations_sync_detail: For every child, global_vars and data_operations contain exactly the same set of resources and operations. No exceptions. This ensures synchronization.
resource_audit.coverage_detail: All resources mentioned in internal_leaf_accesses (patients, appointments) are covered in global_vars and data_operations. No resource is missing.
